vocab.word: the grass is always greener on the other side
vocab.ipa: /ðə ɡrɑːs ɪz ˈɔːlweɪz ˈɡriːnə(r) ɒn ðɪ ˈʌðə(r) saɪd/
vocab.meaning: đứng núi này trông núi nọ (luôn thấy thứ ở xa đẹp hơn)
vocab.example: Perhaps I prefer foreign landscapes simply because the grass is always greener on the other side, even though my country is stunning.
I lean towards views in other countries because a change of scenery is essential for me to feel truly recharged.

vocab.meaning: thưởng ngoạn/chiêm ngưỡng vẻ đẹp (một cách say mê)
vocab.example: I love traveling to Europe just to feast my eyes on the grand gothic architecture that we don't have back home.

vocab.meaning: khởi hành/lên đường đi du lịch
vocab.example: Whenever I hit the road in my own country, I’m constantly surprised by the hidden vistas I’ve overlooked.

vocab.meaning: ngắm cảnh/tham quan những địa danh nổi tiếng
vocab.example: I prefer to see the sights abroad because it allows me to witness landmarks that I’ve only ever seen on a screen.

vocab.meaning: thế giới rộng mở/có vô vàn cơ hội để khám phá
vocab.example: With so many budget airlines now, the world is my oyster, and I’d rather explore diverse views across the globe.

vocab.meaning: tuyệt vời/đẹp ngoài sức tưởng tượng
vocab.example: The mountain views in my own country are truly out of this world, especially during the sunrise.

vocab.meaning: bình thường/không có gì đặc sắc
vocab.example: To be honest, the views in my city are a bit middle of the road, which is why I crave the dramatic cliffs of other countries.

vocab.word: beauty is in the eye of the beholder
vocab.ipa: /ˈbjuːti ɪz ɪn ðɪ aɪ əv ðə bɪˈhəʊldə(r)/
vocab.meaning: vẻ đẹp nằm trong mắt kẻ si tình (tùy cảm nhận mỗi người)
vocab.example: Beauty is in the eye of the beholder, but personally, I find the ruggedness of foreign deserts more captivating than our local forests.

vocab.meaning: nỗ lực vượt bậc (đi xa hơn để đạt được điều gì)
vocab.example: I’m willing to go the extra mile and travel for hours to witness a view that is completely different from what I see every day.
